title: The Coinage of Sixty Rupees and Ten Rupees Coins to Commemorate the occasion of Diamond Jubilee of Coir Board Rules, 2014

“भारत” श瀑द और दा猆 प琇रिध पर अं灡ेजी म犆 “INDIA” श瀑द होगा । 琐सह शीष榁 के नीचे 牱पया का 灹तीक '' ' और अंतरा榁玘ीय अंक犘 म犆 अं琈कत मू瀖य “60” भी होगा । पृ玙 पृ玙 पृ玙 पृ玙 भाग भाग भाग भाग िस猳े के इस भाग पर म瀇य म犆, (4) साठ 牱पए 35 灡ाम रजत के िलए 1/500 तक कम या 榖यादा अथा榁त् रजत क琉 अंतव榁瀡तु 灹ित एक हजार म犆 498 से 502 तक हो सकेगी 1/100 तक कम या 榖यादा अथा榁त् वजन 34.65 灡ाम से 35.35 灡ाम तक अंतर हो सकता है । दस 牱पए 7.71 灡ाम (बा琅 वलय, 4.45 灡ाम और के瀈灶ीय भाग 3.26 灡ाम) बा琅 वलय (ए瀖युमीिनयम कां瀡य) ए瀖युमीिनयम - 5.5 से 6.5 ,िनकल - 1.75 से 2.25 और शेष तांबा है । के瀈灶ीय भाग - (तांबा िनकेल) तांबा और िनकेल दोन犘 के िलए 1 तक कम या 榖यादा, अथा榁त तांबा 74 से 76 तक और िनकेल 24 से 26 तक का हो सकेगा । 2.5 तक कम या 榖यादा अथा榁त् वजन म犆 7.52 灡ाम से 7.90 灡ाम तक अंतर हो सकता है* [फा. सं. 02/15/2013-िस猳ा] पंकज गु玅ा, अवर सिचव (एसपीएमसी और िस猳ा) MINISTRY OF FINANCE (Department of Economic Affairs) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 20th February, 2014 G.S.R. 102 (E).— In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (1) read with clause (d) and clause (e) of sub-section (2) of section 24 of the Coinage Act, 2011 (11 of 2011), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules, namely:— 1. Short title and commencement. — (i) These rules may be called “The coinage of Sixty Rupees and Ten Rupees coins to commemorate the occasion of Diamond Jubilee of Coir Board, Rules, 2014”. (ii) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Denominations, Dimensions etc,.—(i) The Coins of the following denominations shall be coined at the Mint for issue under the authority of the Central Government to commemorate the occasion of “Diamond Jubilee of Coir Board”, namely:— (i) Sixty Rupees; (ii) Ten Rupees. -- 3 of 5 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] (ii) The coins of the above denominations shall conform to the following dimensions, designs and composition, namely:— Denomination of the coin Shape and outside diameter Number of serrations Metal composition (1) ( 2 ) (3) (4) Sixty Rupees Circular 44 millimeters 200 Q Quaternary Alloy Silver - 50 per cent. Copper - 40 per cent. Nickel - 5 per cent. Zinc - 5 per cent. Ten Rupees Circular 27 millimeters (Bi-metallic) Nil Outer Ring (Aluminum Bronze) Copper - 92 per cent. Aluminium - 6 per cent. Nickel - 2 per cent. Center Piece (Cupro Nickel) Copper - 75 per cent. Nickel - 25 per cent. DESIGN 2. Sixty Rupees Obverse The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capital of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend “स瀄यमेव जयते” inscribed below, flanked on the left periphery with the word “भारत“ in Devnagri script and on the right periphery flanked with the word “INDIA” in English. It shall also bear the rupee symbol “`” and the denominational value “60” in international numerals below the Lion Capital. Reverse The face of the coin shall bear the logo of “Coir Board” in the centre, with the inscription “DIAMOND JUBILEE” in English above the logo and the year “1953-2013” shall be inscribed below the logo. The logo and the words mentioned above are encircled in design of coir rope. The words “कयर बोड/ के 60 वष/” in Devnagri shall be flanked on upper periphery and “60 YEARS OF COIR BOARD” in English on the lower periphery. -- 4 of 5 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3(i)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 5 3. Ten Rupees Obverse The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capital of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend “स瀄य स瀄य स瀄य स瀄यमेव मेव मेव मेव जयते जयते जयते जयते” inscribed below, flanked on the left periphery with the word “भारत भारत भारत भारत ” in Devnagri script and on the right periphery flanked with the word “INDIA” in English. It shall also bear the rupee symbol “`” and denominational value “10” in International numerals below the Lion Capital. Reverse The face of the coin shall bear the logo of “Coir Board” in the centre, with inscription “DIAMOND JUBILEE” in English above the logo and the year “1953-2013” shall be inscribed below the logo. The logo and the words mentioned above are encircled in design of coir rope. The words “कयर बोड/ के 60 वष/“ in Devnagri shall be flanked on upper periphery and “60 YEARS OF COIR BOARD” in English on the lower periphery. 3. STANDARD WEIGHT AND REMEDY ALLOWED. — The standard weight of the coins of the following denominations, coined to commemorate the occasion of “60 YEARS OF COIR BOARD” and the remedy allowed in making of such coins shall be as specified in the following Table, namely: — TABLE Denomination of coin Standard weight Remedy allowed In composition In standard weight (1) (2) (3 ) (4) Sixty Rupees 35 grams 1/500th plus or minus for silver, that is to say the Silver content may vary from 498 to 502 per 1000. 1/100th plus or minus that is to say, the weight could vary from 34.65 grams to 35.35 grams. Ten Rupees 7.71 grams (Outer Ring 4.45 grams and Centre Piece 3.26 grams) Outer Ring (Aluminium Bronze) Aluminium-5.5 per cent to 6.5 per cent Nickel- 1.75 per cent to 2.25 per cent and remainder are Copper. Centre Piece (Cupro-Nickel) Plus or Minus 1 per cent for both Copper and Nickel, that is to say, the Copper may vary from 74 per cent to 76 per cent and Nickel may vary from 24 percent to 26 per cent. Plus or minus 2.5% that is to say, weight could vary from 7.52 grams to 7.90 grams. [F. No.02/15/2013-Coin] PANKAJ GUPTA, Under Secy.
